title: Reconquesta
description: Reconquesta is a medieval, tactical collectable card game where you, as a Majesty, Recruit Conquerors to siege rival realms and reduce their Glory Points to zero.
instructions: On your turn, draw a card, Pillage Resources, then Recruit, move, and battle with your Conquerors on the Battlefield, using Actions, Artillery, and Defenses to push into enemy zones and strike the opposing Majesty’s Glory Points. An interactive tutorial and full 'how to play' guide are available in-game to walk you step-by-step through your first Reconquesta battles.
knownIssues: Action cards are not playable in response to your opponent's actions.
Some parts of the Interactive Tutorial are buggy.

title: Rankers
description: Rankers is a strategic card game where players race to complete a communal pyramid — from Rank 8 up to King Ranker — by matching arrow-support rules, with Power-up cards adding tactical twists.
instructions: Players start with 5 cards and build a shared pyramid upward from Rank 8, playing cards only when arrows from the row below 'support' the space above, with single arrows dictating left/right placement. Each turn, draw back up to 5 cards, then take 3 actions — playing Rank cards, using Power-ups (Draw 2, Peek & Take, Pyramid Grab), or discarding — and reshuffle the discard pile if the draw pile ever runs out. Power-ups are removed from the game after the 5th Rank is completed. The first player to place the King Ranker at the top wins.
knownIssues: The size of cards in hand may cause some UI issues and inconveniences.

title: Junban 順番
description: Create numerical order from chaos in this peaceful, meditative, and mindful slide puzzle inspired dice game.
instructions: Slide the dice to arrange them in ascending numerical order from top-left to bottom-right, with matching values placed next to each other in the sequence and the empty space ending in the bottom-right corner. Tap or click any die adjacent to the blank space to slide it into the gap. Complete the puzzle by getting every die in the correct order and position — the fewer moves and the less time it takes, the better.
knownIssues: Instructions may not be clear for a first time player.

title: Jaywalking
description: Jaywalking is a Frogger inspired strategy and memory game where 1–6 players dodge pixel-art traffic across 16 lanes of road, betting on safe paths while avoiding cars that send them back to start.
instructions: Each round, all players simultaneously choose to move -2, -1, 0, +1, or +2 lanes toward the finish line, then one road card is revealed per lane showing either a safe road, a pothole, or a car. Any player whose lane reveals a car is sent all the way back to the start. The first player to cross all 16 lanes and reach the finish line wins!
knownIssues: Lots of UI and UX issues.
Difficult to track which lane set is relevant each round.
Character images may not load properly.
